article_meta.title: Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11

transcript.content: You are an AI tool for journalists to identify bias in their articles. Output ONLY a valid JSON array. No prose, no markdown fences. Empty array [] if no bias found. Format: [{"biasType": "...", "biasedText": "...", "explanation": "..."}]

Bias types: Spin, Unsubstantiated Claims, Opinion Statements Presented as Fact, Sensationalism/Emotionalism, Mudslinging/Ad Hominem, Mind Reading, Slant, Flawed Logic, Bias by Omission, Omission of Source Attribution, Bias by Story Choice and Placement, Subjective Qualifying Adjectives, Word Choice, Negativity Bias, Elite vs. Populist Bias.

Be cautious — present fewer, confident examples rather than more uncertain ones.

Analyze this article:

---
HEADLINE: Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11
SOURCE: Breitbart Fact Check

ARTICLE:

Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11 Feb 2026 Kid Rock buried Puerto Rican rapper Bad Bunny in the gossip site TMZ's poll asking fans their feelings about the 2026 Super Bowl Halftime show, with Rock nearly doubling Bad Bunny's support. The poll may not have gone exactly how TMZ expected as Kid Rock soared ahead of Bad Bunny with 64.5 percent saying Rock had the better show to Bad Bunny's 35.5 percent before TMZ shut the poll down. TMZ tried its best to lead its fans to pick Bad Bunny by characterizing the rabidly anti-Trump rapper's show as taking place on "one of the world's biggest stages," while merely saying that Kid Rock "tried to outshine" Bad Bunny. The site also frontloaded the poll by putting Bad Bunny's name first, writing, "Who had the better halftime show — Bad Bunny or Kid Rock?" Fans did not fall for the push… Many fans jumped on TMZ's X post to joke that the poll probably wasn't going in the direction the gossip site thought it would. According to some of the latest statistics, it seems that nearly half of Super Bowl viewers tuned out of the league's Bad Bunny Halftime Show. Finally, in a strange turn of events, rapper Bad Bunny wiped all his social media accounts immediately after his Super Bowl performance. Follow Warner Todd Huston on Facebook at: Facebook.com/Warner.Todd.Huston, Truth Social @WarnerToddHuston, or at X/Twitter @WTHuston EntertainmentPoliticsSportsBad BunnyKid RockSuper BowlTMZTurning Point USA
---

parsed_output.biasedText: Kid Rock buried Puerto Rican rapper Bad Bunny
parsed_output.explanation: The verb 'buried' is loaded language that frames the poll result as a crushing defeat rather than using neutral terms like 'defeated' or 'led.' This word choice carries negative connotations and sensationalizes the result.

parsed_output.biasedText: The poll may not have gone exactly how TMZ expected
parsed_output.explanation: This framing imputes a motive to TMZ without evidence, suggesting they had a preferred outcome and were disappointed. It spins the story to create a narrative of TMZ's alleged bias rather than simply reporting poll results.

parsed_output.biasedText: TMZ tried its best to lead its fans to pick Bad Bunny
parsed_output.explanation: This claims to know TMZ's intentions and motives without evidence. The article assumes TMZ deliberately designed the poll to favor Bad Bunny rather than presenting evidence of such intent.

parsed_output.biasedText: Fans did not fall for the push
parsed_output.explanation: This assumes as fact that TMZ was trying to manipulate fans and that fans consciously resisted this manipulation, without providing evidence for either claim.
The word 'rabidly' is an emotionally charged, subjective descriptor that's unnecessary to the story and serves to negatively characterize Bad Bunny based on political views.

parsed_output.biasedText: nearly half of Super Bowl viewers tuned out of the league's Bad Bunny Halftime Show
parsed_output.explanation: This statistic lacks proper context or source attribution. No comparison is provided to typical halftime show viewership drops, which are common regardless of performer.
